steps.title: Spot the verb phrase and parallel element
steps.content: Before the blank, the sentence contains the modal verb phrase “may be” which expects a past‑participle complement. After the blank, the clause continues with “but also by vapor streams,” a parallel prepositional phrase introduced by the correlative conjunction “but also.”

steps.title: Apply punctuation rule for parallel structure
steps.content: The rule is that no punctuation is needed between the two parts of a correlative conjunction joining phrases. Choices A, B, and C each insert unnecessary punctuation (dashes or commas) that breaks the parallel structure. That matches choice D — D is correct.
